summary:
The primary characteristic noted about impression compounds does not support remaining distortion-free; instead, these materials typically become rigid after curing but need proper application techniques to minimize deformation during use. Modern formulations emphasize balance among setting time control, accuracy retention post-setting, alongside sufficient malleability before final hardening stages. Given this context,

The correct answer should reflect understanding around how such substances behave under typical dental procedures without assuming inflexible permanence lacking practical exceptions or nuances highlighted here today concerning their dynamic nature throughout preparation phases until fully hardened states are achieved accurately reflecting real-world applications where slight adjustments might still be necessary even if initially mixed correctly according instructions provided by manufacturers themselves.
